idea.hook: Why This Asteroid Sample Mission Matters
facts: OSIRIS-REx returned NASA's first asteroid sample to Earth from Bennu on September 24, 2023.
Asteroid samples matter because they preserve material from the early solar system.
idea.payoff: OSIRIS-REx returned NASA's first asteroid sample to Earth from Bennu on September 24, 2023.

upload.title: Why This Asteroid Sample Mission Matters
upload.description:
This is real sample-return mission footage, showing the spacecraft work behind bringing ancient asteroid material back to Earth.

OSIRIS-REx returned NASA's first asteroid sample to Earth from Bennu on September 24, 2023.

Source clip: Our First Asteroid Sample Return Mission is Back on Earth on This Week @NASA – September 29, 2023
NASA source page: https://images.nasa.gov/details/Our First Asteroid Sample Return Mission is Back on Earth on This Week @NASA – September 29, 2023
Uses NASA source media and public information. Do not imply NASA endorsed the finished video or AI output.

leader_kit.pinnedComment: OSIRIS-REx returned NASA's first asteroid sample to Earth from Bennu on September 24, 2023. What should this lesson explain next?
replyTemplates: This mission matters because Bennu material is a physical sample, not just a telescope observation.
Sample-return footage is powerful because the science continues after landing, once the material reaches the lab.
